skill.name: ui-skills
skill.description: Opinionated, evolving constraints to guide agents when building interfaces
skill.summary: Opinionated, evolving constraints to guide agents when building interfaces
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The hardcoded URL findings at SKILL.md lines 4 and 22 are source repository references, not executable network requests. The weak cryptographic algorithm findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 20 are false positives from plain descriptive text; no cryptographic code, commands, prompt injection, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URL
low_findings.description: SKILL.md includes a source repository URL and a Markdown link to the same repository. These links are informational references and do not execute network requests or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected URLs are visible metadata and documentation links. There is no code path, command, or instruction that fetches these URLs or sends user data.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Match Dismissed
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns in descriptive lines. The audited file contains prose about interface guidance, not cryptographic functions, hashes, ciphers, or security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only descriptive skill text on the flagged lines. No weak algorithm name is used in a security context and no cryptographic operation exists.

content.value_statement: AI agents can produce uneven interfaces without clear design constraints. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concise guidance for building more consistent UI work.
actual_capabilities: Provides high-level constraints for interface design work.
Guides agents toward consistent frontend decisions.
Applies to web app and product interface tasks.
Encourages opinionated review of layout and interaction choices.
Points users to the upstream ui-skills source repository.
limitations: Does not include executable code, scripts, or generated assets.
Does not define a complete design system or component library.
Does not inspect screenshots or validate visual output by itself.
Requires the agent to apply the guidance during a UI task.

use_cases.title: Shape New Product Screens
use_cases.description: Use the skill to keep interface decisions focused while creating dashboards, settings pages, or workflow screens.

use_cases.title: Review AI-Generated UI
use_cases.description: Apply the constraints to evaluate whether an agent-built interface feels coherent and usable.

use_cases.title: Guide Design Iteration
use_cases.description: Use the guidance as a shared baseline when asking an agent to refine spacing, hierarchy, and controls.

prompt_templates.title: Apply UI Guidance
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple interface task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ui-skills guidance while designing this screen. Focus on clear hierarchy, practical controls, and a polished layout.

prompt_templates.title: Review This Interface
prompt_templates.scenario: Check an existing AI-built UI for design issues.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this interface using the ui-skills constraints. Identify layout, control, spacing, and interaction issues that should be fixed.

prompt_templates.title: Improve A Workflow Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve this workflow screen with ui-skills guidance. Preserve the current functionality and make the interface easier to scan and use.

prompt_templates.title: Set Design Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a full feature build.
prompt_templates.prompt: Before implementing this feature, apply ui-skills as design direction. Define the screen structure, expected controls, responsive behavior, and review criteria.

output_examples.input: Design a settings page for a team workspace.
output_examples.output: A structured settings screen with grouped controls, clear section headings, predictable toggles, and compact actions for common team tasks.

output: The main metrics should be easier to compare at a glance.
Secondary actions should move out of primary button positions.
Table controls need clearer filtering and sorting affordances.

output_examples.output: A focused sequence with concise copy, stable button placement, readable form controls, and fewer competing visual treatments.

best_practices: Use the skill before starting UI implementation so design constraints guide structure.
Pair the guidance with concrete product goals, target users, and screen requirements.
Review the final interface against hierarchy, controls, spacing, and responsive behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the skill as a complete design system or brand guide.
Do not rely on it to perform automated visual testing.
Do not use it as a substitute for user research or accessibility review.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI agents make more consistent interface design decisions during frontend work.

faq.question: Does it generate UI components directly?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance that an agent can apply while creating or reviewing components.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found only descriptive text and repository links, with no executable behavior.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. The file includes repository links, but it does not instruct the agent to fetch or send data.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Frontend developers, designers, and product teams can use it to guide AI-assisted interface work.
